basics.name: Lucas Skog
basics.label: Full-stack developer
basics.summary: Full-stack developer building AI-driven products — from local agents to complete SaaS platforms.

projects.name: Meritvo
projects.description: AI-assisted CV platform. A complete SaaS for building, tailoring and exporting CVs. Every experience is stored once in a language-neutral masterdata model — then any number of CVs can be created, each tailored to a specific role. Private beta.
highlights: AI proposes — you approve. Every AI edit goes through a review queue; nothing is silently rewritten.
Paste a job ad to get a match score, keywords, gaps and concrete recommendations.
PDF/HTML/TXT export from a shared render model — preview and export are always identical.

projects.name: Pilot
projects.description: Local-first AI agent that verifies its own work. A tool-using agent that runs fully local on Windows via Ollama. It classifies each turn, gathers evidence — reads files, runs shell commands, does web research — and answers only from what it has actually verified. Open source.
highlights: Local-first: runs on Ollama models with no cloud — OpenAI as an optional backend.
Reproducible eval suite: capabilities are measured, not claimed.
Layered safety model with timeouts, tokens and loopback by default.

projects.name: Design-Pilot
projects.description: Site builder with canvas and CLI — you own the code. A shadcn-inspired site builder in two parts: a Figma-like canvas where real React components render live, and a CLI that copies production-ready component code into your project. No npm dependency — the code is yours. In development.
highlights: Drag and drop real React components, edit props per instance, nest freely.
Build multiple pages and layouts, wire internal links, export the whole project.
AI assistant via a local proxy — the API key never leaves the machine.

projects.name: Viska
projects.description: Dictation anywhere on Windows — no cloud. Press F9 and speak: Swedish-trained KB-Whisper runs on a local GPU and types wherever the cursor is, in any Windows app. Audio never leaves the machine and transcription takes under a second. Private beta.
highlights: Live mode on GPU: finished phrases are typed while you're still speaking.
F8 polishes selected text with a local Qwen3 model — template library and per-app profiles.
Privacy in the details: pasted text stays out of the Win+V history and cloud clipboard.

projects.name: referat
projects.description: Meeting minutes that never leave your machine. Records the meeting, transcribes it and writes finished minutes — summary, decisions and action items. Everything stored locally, API keys encrypted with Windows DPAPI. Open source.
highlights: Captures system audio and microphone directly — no bot joins the call.
Speaker diarization via a local pyannote server: who said what.
Choose where the AI runs — fully local, or any OpenAI-compatible/Anthropic endpoint.

projects.name: Smask!
projects.description: The family cookbook — tested and approved recipes. Stores the recipes the family actually cooked — with ratings, notes and photos — and turns the week's recipes into a ready shopping list. Private beta.
highlights: AI ingestion: photograph a cookbook page, paste a link or dictate the recipe.
Shopping list that merges ingredients across recipes and groups by store section.
Image pipeline with three webp variants and portion scaling right in the recipe view.

projects.name: Stege
projects.description: Chess-puzzle PWA — a 498-level climber ladder plus time trial over 19,000 Lichess puzzles, zero backend. Live.

projects.name: Rep Counter
projects.description: Mobile-first PWA for logging exercise reps — quick buttons, daily goals, an activity heatmap. All data stays on the device. Live.
